partial_progress.details: Completed analysis of DirectMultiFamilyBFMCS implementation. Found architectural limitation in BFMCS Int design. Remaining sorries are mathematically necessary for the current architecture. Recommendations provided.

key_findings: BFMCS modal_forward assumes all families share SAME MCS at each time t
DirectMultiFamilyBFMCS has DIFFERENT MCS per family at same time - architectural mismatch
Cross-family modal_forward (Box phi in M => phi in N) is NOT syntactically provable in S5
TM logic HAS full S5 axioms (T, 4, B, 5-collapse) but this doesn't help
modal_backward at t=0 is correctly sorry-free via saturation
F/P sorries are fundamental dovetailing gap (separate issue)

choice_points.name: Use CanonicalMCS Domain
choice_points.description: Replace BFMCS Int with BFMCS CanonicalMCS

summary.recommendation: Use CanonicalMCS domain (Choice Point 2) for sorry-free modal coherence. The Int-indexed approach has fundamental architectural incompatibility with cross-family modal_forward.
